slides.title: Break and Continue 🛑
slides.content:
# Controlling Loops

**`break`** – exits the loop immediately.
**`continue`** – skips the rest of the current iteration and jumps to the next.

Useful for early termination or skipping certain values.

Example:
```dart
for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
  if (i == 3) continue; // skip 3
  if (i == 7) break;    // stop at 7
  print(i);
}
```
Prints: 0,1,2,4,5,6

quizData.explanation: When i=2, continue skips the print, so 2 is not printed.

slides.title: See it in Action
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Use break and continue sparingly

They can make code harder to follow. Sometimes restructuring the loop condition is clearer.
